rubric.established: Comprehensive IT spending transparency with consolidated reporting across units.
rubric.optimizing: Real-time IT spending dashboards with automated categorization and anomaly detection.

items.text: How effectively does the organization track IT spending against the IT portfolio?

rubric.initial: IT spending is not linked to portfolio items.
rubric.developing: Some linking exists for major projects.
rubric.established: All IT spending is tracked against portfolio items with variance analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Automated spending-portfolio alignment with predictive cost modeling and optimization.

items.text: To what extent does the organization report IT spending per FITARA requirements?

rubric.initial: FITARA spending reporting requirements are not met.
rubric.developing: Partial reporting exists with significant gaps.
rubric.established: Full FITARA-compliant IT spending reports are produced on schedule.
rubric.optimizing: Automated FITARA reporting with real-time compliance monitoring and continuous improvement.

items.text: How well does the organization manage shadow IT spending?

rubric.initial: Shadow IT spending is unknown and unmanaged.
rubric.developing: Some awareness of shadow IT exists but no management.
rubric.established: Shadow IT is identified, quantified, and managed through governance processes.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive shadow IT management with automated detection and governance integration.

items.text: How effectively does the organization use IT spending data for decision-making?

rubric.initial: IT spending data does not inform decisions.
rubric.developing: Some data is referenced but analysis is limited.
rubric.established: IT spending analytics drive investment decisions and optimization.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced spending analytics with benchmarking and predictive modeling drive strategic allocation.

items.text: How mature is the organization's IT investment portfolio selection process?

rubric.initial: No portfolio selection process exists.
rubric.developing: Informal selection occurs without consistent criteria.
rubric.established: Formal portfolio selection uses defined criteria with stakeholder input.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic portfolio selection with optimization models and real-time strategic alignment.

items.text: How effectively does the organization control IT investments through stage gates?

rubric.initial: No stage-gate controls exist for IT investments.
rubric.developing: Some reviews occur but stage gates are informal.
rubric.established: Formal stage-gate reviews with defined criteria control IT investment progression.
rubric.optimizing: Automated stage-gate management with real-time performance data and decision support.

items.text: To what extent does the organization manage the IT investment portfolio as a whole?

rubric.initial: Investments are managed individually with no portfolio view.
rubric.developing: A portfolio view exists but management is fragmented.
rubric.established: Active portfolio management balances investments across strategic themes.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic portfolio optimization continuously rebalances based on performance and strategic shifts.

items.text: How well does the organization evaluate alternative investment options?

rubric.initial: Alternatives are not evaluated for IT investments.
rubric.developing: Some alternatives are discussed informally.
rubric.established: Formal alternative analysis with cost-benefit evaluation is standard practice.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive option analysis with scenario modeling and sensitivity testing.

items.text: How effectively does the organization align IT investments with federal strategic priorities?

rubric.initial: IT investments are not aligned with federal priorities.
rubric.developing: Some alignment exists for major initiatives.
rubric.established: All IT investments are evaluated against federal strategic priorities.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous alignment monitoring with automated strategic fit scoring and reporting.

items.text: How well does the organization develop business cases for IT investments?

rubric.initial: No business cases are developed.
rubric.developing: Informal justifications exist for some investments.
rubric.established: Formal business cases with defined templates and review processes are standard.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive business cases with advanced financial modeling and sensitivity analysis.

items.text: How effectively does the organization track ROI of IT investments post-implementation?

rubric.initial: ROI is not tracked after implementation.
rubric.developing: Some post-implementation reviews occur.
rubric.established: Formal ROI tracking compares actual to projected returns for all significant investments.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous ROI monitoring with automated variance analysis and portfolio-level value tracking.

items.text: To what extent does the organization use standardized financial metrics for IT investment?

rubric.initial: No standardized financial metrics are used.
rubric.developing: Some metrics are used inconsistently.
rubric.established: Standardized metrics (NPV, IRR, payback period) are consistently applied.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced financial analytics with risk-adjusted returns and option valuation.

items.text: How well does the organization account for non-financial benefits in business cases?

rubric.initial: Only financial benefits are considered.
rubric.developing: Non-financial benefits are mentioned but not quantified.
rubric.established: Non-financial benefits are systematically identified and valued in business cases.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive value frameworks quantify strategic, operational, and social benefits.

items.text: How effectively does the organization learn from past investment outcomes?

rubric.initial: No learning from past investments occurs.
rubric.developing: Occasional reviews of failed projects exist.
rubric.established: Systematic post-investment reviews capture lessons and improve future business cases.
rubric.optimizing: Investment outcome analytics with pattern recognition drive continuous improvement of selection.

items.text: How well does the organization develop IT acquisition strategies?

rubric.initial: No IT acquisition strategies exist.
rubric.developing: Strategies are developed ad-hoc for major purchases.
rubric.established: Formal acquisition strategies align with architecture and portfolio priorities.
rubric.optimizing: Strategic acquisition planning with market intelligence and innovative procurement approaches.

items.text: How effectively does the organization use modular contracting for IT?

rubric.initial: Modular contracting is not used.
rubric.developing: Some modular approaches exist but are not standard.
rubric.established: Modular contracting is standard practice for IT acquisitions.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced modular acquisition with agile contracting and continuous delivery models.

items.text: To what extent does the organization assess vendor market capabilities before acquisition?

rubric.initial: No market assessment is performed.
rubric.developing: Informal market awareness exists.
rubric.established: Formal market research and vendor capability assessment inform acquisition strategy.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous market intelligence with vendor landscape analysis drives strategic sourcing.

items.text: How well does the organization manage IT acquisition risk?

rubric.initial: Acquisition risk is not assessed.
rubric.developing: Some risk awareness exists for major acquisitions.
rubric.established: Formal risk assessment is part of all IT acquisition strategies.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive acquisition risk management with mitigation strategies and contingency planning.

items.text: How effectively does the organization align IT acquisitions with enterprise architecture?

rubric.initial: Acquisitions are not aligned with architecture.
rubric.developing: Some reference to architecture exists.
rubric.established: All IT acquisitions are validated against enterprise architecture standards.
rubric.optimizing: Architecture-driven acquisition with automated compliance validation and strategic fit analysis.

items.text: How mature is the organization's IT contract management process?

rubric.initial: No formal IT contract management exists.
rubric.developing: Basic contract tracking exists but management is reactive.
rubric.established: Comprehensive contract management covers lifecycle from negotiation to closeout.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent contract management with automated compliance, renewal, and performance tracking.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage IT contract performance?

rubric.initial: Contract performance is not monitored.
rubric.developing: Some monitoring occurs for major contracts.
rubric.established: All significant IT contracts have defined performance metrics with regular reviews.
rubric.optimizing: Real-time contract performance dashboards with automated variance alerts and remediation.

items.text: To what extent does the organization manage IT contract compliance?

rubric.initial: Contract compliance is not tracked.
rubric.developing: Compliance is reviewed during audits only.
rubric.established: Ongoing compliance monitoring ensures adherence to contract terms.
rubric.optimizing: Automated compliance monitoring with real-time alerts and remediation tracking.

items.text: How well does the organization negotiate favorable IT contract terms?

rubric.initial: No negotiation strategy exists.
rubric.developing: Basic negotiation occurs without strategic approach.
rubric.established: Structured negotiation with defined strategies achieves favorable terms.
rubric.optimizing: Strategic negotiation with market intelligence and benchmarking optimizes contract value.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage IT contract transitions and exits?

rubric.initial: Contract transitions are chaotic with no planning.
rubric.developing: Some transition planning exists for major contracts.
rubric.established: Formal transition and exit planning is included in all significant contracts.
rubric.optimizing: Proactive transition management with knowledge transfer and continuity assurance.

items.text: How mature is the organization's cybersecurity program?

rubric.initial: No formal cybersecurity program exists.
rubric.developing: Basic security measures are in place but program is immature.
rubric.established: A comprehensive cybersecurity program aligned with NIST CSF or similar framework.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced cybersecurity program with threat intelligence, zero trust, and continuous improvement.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage security risk assessment?

rubric.initial: Security risk assessments are not performed.
rubric.developing: Ad-hoc risk assessments occur for select systems.
rubric.established: Regular security risk assessments cover all critical systems with formal methodology.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous security risk assessment with automated scanning and real-time risk scoring.

items.text: To what extent does the organization implement security controls across the enterprise?

rubric.initial: No standardized security controls exist.
rubric.developing: Some controls are implemented inconsistently.
rubric.established: Comprehensive security controls are implemented based on risk and framework requirements.
rubric.optimizing: Automated security control deployment with continuous monitoring and adaptive enforcement.

items.text: How well does the organization manage security awareness and training?

rubric.initial: No security awareness program exists.
rubric.developing: Annual security awareness training is provided.
rubric.established: Comprehensive security awareness with role-based training and phishing simulations.
rubric.optimizing: Adaptive security awareness with behavioral analytics and continuous engagement.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage security metrics and reporting?

rubric.initial: Security metrics are not tracked.
rubric.developing: Basic metrics exist but reporting is inconsistent.
rubric.established: Comprehensive security metrics with regular executive reporting.
rubric.optimizing: Real-time security dashboards with risk-quantified reporting and trend analysis.

items.text: How mature is the organization's cybersecurity incident response capability?

rubric.initial: No incident response capability exists.
rubric.developing: Basic response occurs but processes are undefined.
rubric.established: Formal incident response plan with trained team and defined procedures.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced incident response with automation, threat hunting, and continuous improvement.

items.text: How effectively does the organization conduct incident response exercises?

rubric.initial: No incident response exercises are conducted.
rubric.developing: Occasional tabletop exercises occur.
rubric.established: Regular exercises including tabletop and simulated attacks test response capabilities.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous response testing with red team exercises and automated simulation.

items.text: To what extent does the organization learn from security incidents?

rubric.initial: No learning from security incidents occurs.
rubric.developing: Post-incident reviews occur for major events.
rubric.established: Formal post-incident analysis drives systemic improvements to security posture.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced incident learning with threat intelligence integration and predictive defense improvement.

items.text: How well does the organization coordinate incident response across teams?

rubric.initial: Incident response is uncoordinated.
rubric.developing: Some coordination exists for major incidents.
rubric.established: Cross-functional incident response coordination with defined roles and communication.
rubric.optimizing: Orchestrated incident response with automated workflows and seamless team coordination.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage cybersecurity recovery processes?

rubric.initial: No cybersecurity recovery processes exist.
rubric.developing: Basic recovery procedures exist for some systems.
rubric.established: Comprehensive recovery processes with tested procedures and defined priorities.
rubric.optimizing: Automated recovery orchestration with resilience validation and rapid restoration capabilities.

items.text: How mature is the state agency's enterprise architecture practice?

rubric.initial: No EA practice exists.
rubric.developing: Informal architecture activities occur without structure.
rubric.established: A formal EA practice with defined roles, processes, and deliverables is operational.
rubric.optimizing: Mature EA practice drives strategic decisions with continuous improvement and innovation.

items.text: How effectively does the EA practice support state IT strategic planning?

rubric.initial: EA does not contribute to strategic planning.
rubric.developing: Some EA artifacts inform planning informally.
rubric.established: EA is a formal input to state IT strategic planning with defined deliverables.
rubric.optimizing: EA drives strategic planning with scenario modeling and capability-based investment prioritization.

items.text: To what extent does the EA practice address all architecture domains?

rubric.initial: No architecture domains are formally addressed.
rubric.developing: One or two domains are partially documented.
rubric.established: All four architecture domains are covered with defined methods and deliverables.
rubric.optimizing: Cross-domain integration with automated consistency checking and impact analysis.

items.text: How well does the organization develop and maintain current and target state architectures?

rubric.initial: Neither current nor target states are documented.
rubric.developing: Some current state exists but target state is absent.
rubric.established: Both current and target states are maintained with roadmaps.
rubric.optimizing: Living architecture models with automated change tracking and gap analysis.

items.text: How effectively does the state agency manage technology standards?

rubric.initial: No technology standards are defined.
rubric.developing: Some informal standards exist.
rubric.established: Formal technology standards are published and governed.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic standards management with automated compliance and community input.

items.text: How well does architecture governance support state IT decision-making?

rubric.initial: No architecture governance exists.
rubric.developing: Informal architecture advice is provided.
rubric.established: Formal architecture governance with review processes and decision authority.
rubric.optimizing: Architecture governance drives strategic technology decisions with measurable impact.

items.text: To what extent does the state agency enforce architecture compliance?

rubric.initial: Architecture compliance is not enforced.
rubric.developing: Some compliance checking occurs for major projects.
rubric.established: All significant projects undergo architecture compliance review.
rubric.optimizing: Automated architecture compliance with continuous monitoring and remediation tracking.

items.text: How comprehensive is the state agency's IT portfolio inventory?

rubric.initial: No IT portfolio inventory exists.
rubric.developing: Partial inventory covers some systems.
rubric.established: Comprehensive portfolio inventory covers all IT assets, projects, and services.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic portfolio inventory with automated discovery and real-time status tracking.

items.text: How effectively does the agency report on portfolio health?

rubric.initial: Portfolio health is not reported.
rubric.developing: Ad-hoc reporting exists for select initiatives.
rubric.established: Regular portfolio health reporting covers status, risk, and value.
rubric.optimizing: Real-time portfolio dashboards with predictive analytics and automated alerts.

items.text: To what extent does the agency track portfolio costs and value?

rubric.initial: Portfolio costs are not tracked.
rubric.developing: Basic cost tracking exists for major projects.
rubric.established: Comprehensive cost and value tracking across the portfolio.
rubric.optimizing: Activity-based costing with value realization tracking and optimization recommendations.

items.text: How mature is the state agency's IT project prioritization process?

rubric.initial: No prioritization process exists.
rubric.developing: Informal prioritization occurs without consistent criteria.
rubric.established: Formal prioritization with defined criteria and stakeholder input.
rubric.optimizing: Data-driven prioritization with optimization modeling and real-time strategic alignment.

items.text: How effectively does the agency measure ROI for IT investments?

rubric.initial: ROI is not measured.
rubric.developing: Rough ROI estimates exist for major projects.
rubric.established: Formal ROI measurement with standardized methodology.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous ROI tracking with automated value attribution and portfolio optimization.

items.text: To what extent does the agency use portfolio data for strategic decisions?

rubric.initial: Portfolio data does not inform decisions.
rubric.developing: Some portfolio data is referenced occasionally.
rubric.established: Portfolio analytics formally inform strategic IT decisions.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive portfolio analytics drive proactive strategic decisions and resource allocation.

items.text: How well does the agency balance the portfolio across maintenance and innovation?

rubric.initial: No portfolio balance framework exists.
rubric.developing: Balance is discussed but not managed.
rubric.established: Formal portfolio balance targets are defined and tracked.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic portfolio rebalancing optimizes investment mix based on strategic priorities and outcomes.

items.text: How effectively is the state agency modernizing citizen-facing digital services?

rubric.initial: No digital service modernization is underway.
rubric.developing: Some services are being updated but no cohesive strategy.
rubric.established: A formal modernization program addresses citizen-facing services with defined priorities.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous service modernization with user research, agile delivery, and measurable outcomes.

items.text: How well does the agency adopt modern development practices for digital services?

rubric.initial: Legacy development practices are used exclusively.
rubric.developing: Some modern practices are adopted inconsistently.
rubric.established: Modern development practices (agile, DevOps, cloud-native) are standard.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced practices with continuous delivery, feature flags, and rapid experimentation.

items.text: To what extent does the agency leverage shared platforms for digital services?

rubric.initial: No shared platforms exist; each service is built independently.
rubric.developing: Some shared components are reused informally.
rubric.established: Shared platforms and services reduce duplication and accelerate delivery.
rubric.optimizing: Platform-as-a-service with self-service capabilities drives rapid digital service deployment.

items.text: How effectively does the agency manage the digital service lifecycle?

rubric.initial: Digital service lifecycle is not managed.
rubric.developing: Some lifecycle events are managed reactively.
rubric.established: Formal lifecycle management covers design through retirement.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous lifecycle management with automated monitoring and proactive modernization triggers.

items.text: How well does the agency ensure digital services are accessible to all citizens?

rubric.initial: Accessibility is not considered.
rubric.developing: Basic accessibility is attempted but compliance is incomplete.
rubric.established: Digital services meet accessibility standards with regular testing.
rubric.optimizing: Accessibility excellence with continuous testing, user research, and inclusive design.

items.text: How effectively does the agency address digital equity in service delivery?

rubric.initial: Digital equity is not considered.
rubric.developing: Some awareness exists but no formal approach.
rubric.established: Digital equity is formally addressed in service design and delivery.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive digital equity strategy ensures all communities can access services.

items.text: To what extent does the agency provide multilingual digital services?

rubric.initial: No multilingual support exists.
rubric.developing: Some translation is available for select services.
rubric.established: Multilingual support is provided for major languages in the community.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive multilingual services with real-time translation and cultural adaptation.
